summary: Large Yoshi special-move decompilation PR centered on ftYs_SpecialS, with smaller SpecialHi and header typing work. It replaced many placeholder stubs with C implementations for Yoshi Egg Roll animation, physics, collision, landing, and helper routines, added typed Yoshi attribute and motion-var fields, and corrected Yoshi thrown-egg item prototypes. The decomp.dev report showed +5484 matched bytes, 9 new 100% matches, and 11 additional unmatched-item improvements.
what_changed: Implemented fn_8012E110 in ftYs_SpecialHi.c, including Yoshi egg creation via it_802B2A10, accessory callback behavior, held item tracking through fp->fv.ys.x2238 and fp->x1984_heldItemSpec, and cleanup of take_dmg_cb/death2_cb after it_802B28C8 is called.
Moved setDamageCallbacks earlier in ftYs_SpecialHi.c so fn_8012E110 can use it, and added lb_00B0.h plus placeholder.h includes needed by the implementation.
Changed ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C to use the project MAX macro instead of a local inline max helper.

Implemented many ftYs_SpecialS.c routines previously represented by /// # stubs, including ftYs_SpecialS_8012EB48, ftYs_SpecialS_8012F0DC, ftYs_SpecialS_8012F35C, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_0_Anim, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_1_Anim, ftYs_SpecialAirSEnd_Anim, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_2_Anim, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_3_Anim, ftYs_SpecialAirSLanding_Anim, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_0_IASA, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_0_Phys, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_1_Phys, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_0_Coll, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_1_Coll, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_2_Coll, and ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_3_Coll.
Added static inline helpers in ftYs_SpecialS.c for callback setup, wall-bounce effects, scale updates, rotation wrapping, and button-pressure checking.
Added effect and camera interactions for Egg Roll behavior, including efSync_Spawn calls for wall bounce, rolling/floor effects, and end effects, plus Camera_80030E44 for wall bounce.
Refined Yoshi collision data representation by changing ftYs_Unk3_803CEDA4 from a Vec3 array-like initializer into a single ftCollisionBox.
Expanded ftYoshiAttributes in types.h by replacing broad padding with named offset fields such as x54, x58, x60, x64, x68, x94 through xC8, xD0, xD4, xD8, xDC, xE4, and xE8.
Expanded struct ftYs_DatAttrs fields x104, x108, x10C, and x110, keeping the struct size static assert at 0x120.
Expanded ftYoshi_MotionVars.specials by replacing padding with concrete fields x4, x8, x28, and x2C.
Corrected ftYs_SpecialS_8012EB48 declarations in ftYs_SpecialHi.h and ftYs_SpecialS.h from unknown params/return to void ftYs_SpecialS_8012EB48(Fighter_GObj* gobj).
Corrected ityoshieggthrow.h prototypes for it_802B28C8 and it_802B2A10 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed Item_GObj/Fighter_GObj/Vec3/f32/s32 signatures.
smart_moves: Recovered struct fields before or alongside function decompilation, making code express dataflow through fp->dat_attrs and fp->mv.ys.specials instead of relying on opaque padding.
Used small static inline helpers to isolate repeated codegen-sensitive behavior, such as scale updates, callback setup, angle wrapping, and button-pressure tests.

Kept callback semantics explicit: ftYoshi_SpecialS_SetCall assigns death2_cb, take_dmg_cb, deal_dmg_cb, and x21F8 consistently for Egg Roll state, while fn_8012E110 clears damage callbacks after egg throw release.
Turned item functions used by Yoshi SpecialHi into typed prototypes, allowing the caller to pass Vec3 velocity/position data and receive an Item_GObj* without placeholder casts.
Modeled visual behavior as part of the matched logic, including joint scale changes, part rotations, hit capsule cycling, effect spawns, and camera shake/effect calls.
Preserved matching-oriented stack/layout hints with PAD_STACK in newly implemented routines.
decomp_lessons: For fighter special moves, matching often depends on typing per-character attribute blocks and motion-vars before implementing the control-flow-heavy animation, physics, and collision callbacks.
When a previously unknown function is used across fighter and item boundaries, fixing the header prototype can remove guesswork and make both caller and callee usage clearer.

Callbacks are part of the decompiled behavior, not just plumbing; restoring take_dmg_cb, death2_cb, deal_dmg_cb, accessory4_cb, and x21F8 assignments can be required for both correctness and matching.
Static inline wrappers can be useful when the target binary appears to have outline-like or reused code patterns but the source needs to control inlining for matching.
Do not leave a local helper shadowing a common project macro if the macro better matches established style or codegen; this PR removed a local max helper in favor of MAX.
Effect and camera calls should be decompiled with their positional math intact, because wall/floor normals, ECB offsets, facing direction, and atan2f all influence matching and game behavior.
Recovering anonymous padding into offset-named fields is acceptable when semantic names are not yet known, as long as offsets and static size assertions remain accurate.
naming_conventions: Yoshi fighter files use the ftYs_ prefix for symbols, e.g. ftYs_SpecialS_8012EB48 and ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_0_Anim.
Unidentified but address-known functions retain fn_8012xxxx names, e.g. fn_8012E110, fn_8012EC7C, fn_8012EDE8, and fn_8012EFC0.
Recovered but semantically unknown fields are kept as offset-style names such as x54, xD8, x2238, and mv.ys.specials.x14.

Helper routines added for readability/matching use descriptive ftYs_SpecialS_* names, such as ftYs_SpecialS_UpdateScale, ftYs_SpecialS_WrapAndSetRotX, ftYs_SpecialS_CheckButtonPressure, and ftYs_SpecialS_SpawnWallBounceEffect.
Item functions retained address-based it_802Bxxxx names while improving prototypes, e.g. it_802B28C8 and it_802B2A10.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK annotations in several newly implemented functions to preserve stack layout for matching.
Used static inline helper functions and an explicit ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C_outline wrapper, suggesting codegen shaping for call/inline behavior.
Used ABS, MAX, M_PI, M_PI_2, and atan2f consistently in motion and effect-angle calculations.
Indexed Vec4 constants by casting to f32* in ftYs_SpecialS_UpdateScale, matching table-like access to ftYs_Unk3_803CED84 and ftYs_Unk3_803CED94.
Normalized rotation angles with while loops around 0 and 2 * M_PI before calling ftPartSetRotX or ftPartSetRotY.
Preserved explicit enum/integer motion IDs and flags such as 0x167, 0x169, 0x16B, and 0x440012 where semantic names were not recovered.
Changed broad padding in types.h into exact float/int fields, enabling direct field loads that likely matched the target instruction sequence.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code at 58.47%, a +0.14% improvement and +5484 bytes.

The decomp.dev bot reported 9 new 100% matches, all in main/melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_SpecialS: ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_3_Anim, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_0_Anim, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_1_Phys, ftYs_SpecialS_8012F0DC, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_2_Anim, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_0_Phys, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_0_IASA, ftYs_SpecialS_8012F35C, and ftYs_SpecialS_8012EB48.
The bot also reported 11 improvements in unmatched items, including near-complete percentages for several ftYs_SpecialS collision/landing/end routines and 94.02% for ftYs_SpecialHi fn_8012E110.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish the near-matching ftYs_SpecialS collision routines reported at 98-99%.
Search for semantic renames of ftYoshiAttributes fields x54 through xE8 after PR 2307.
Search for later cleanup or naming of fn_8012E110, fn_8012EC7C, fn_8012EDE8, and fn_8013295C.
Check whether ftYs_SpecialS_SpawnWallBounceEffect or ftYs_SpecialS_CheckButtonPressure remained static inline or were refactored in later Yoshi work.
Search item PRs around it_802B28C8 and it_802B2A10 for callee-side decompilation and confirmation of the typed prototypes.
